extra.reasoning:
The property "globally (opted_out implies not sent)" is VIOLATED, reachable within the 15-event bound (14 events used).

The dangerous rule is [t_resume_glitch]: on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 12): sent := true. Because stage maxes at 12, reaching stage 12 then opting out then attempting a send sets sent=true while opted_out=true — exactly the forbidden state. The normal block rule [t_send_blocked] only fires when stage < 12, so it does not protect the maxed-out lock.

The 12-key unlock combination (each adv_N transition's required key from stage N to N+1) is:
  stage 0->1 key_2 (adv_0)
  stage 1->2 key_1 (adv_1)
  stage 2->3 key_0 (adv_2)
  stage 3->4 key_0 (adv_3)
  stage 4->5 key_2 (adv_4)
  stage 5->6 key_2 (adv_5)
  stage 6->7 key_2 (adv_6)
  stage 7->8 key_2 (adv_7)
  stage 8->9 key_0 (adv_8)
  stage 9->10 key_1 (adv_9)
  stage 10->11 key_0 (adv_10)
  stage 11->12 key_2 (adv_11)

Each adv sets sent:=false (and any wrong key at a stage resets stage to 0, so the sequence must be exactly correct, which it is here). Trace:
  init: opted_out=F, sent=F, stage=0
  key_2 -> stage1; key_1 -> stage2; key_0 -> stage3; key_0 -> stage4; key_2 -> stage5; key_2 -> stage6; key_2 -> stage7; key_2 -> stage8; key_0 -> stage9; key_1 -> stage10; key_0 -> stage11; key_2 -> stage12
  optout -> opted_out=T, sent=F, stage=12 (stage unchanged)
  send_attempt -> opted_out=T and stage>=12, so [t_resume_glitch] fires: sent:=T

Final state: opted_out=T AND sent=T. This is a reachable state where opted_out is true but sent is true, violating the safety property (a message is sent after opt-out).
